tipsAndEssentialsSubtitle: Train smarter, eat better, recover faster.
tip_training_1_title: Warm up for at least 5 minutes
tip_training_1_desc: Raises heart rate and preps joints, cutting injury risk by up to 50%.
tip_training_2_desc: Squat, deadlift, bench press, and overhead press build the foundation for everything else.
tip_training_3_desc: Add small weight or reps each week. 1kg more per month compounds into serious strength.
tip_training_4_desc: Three moderate sessions a week for a year beats a month of daily grind then burnout.
tip_training_5_title: Train with full range of motion
tip_training_5_desc: Full ROM activates more muscle fibers and improves flexibility over time.
tip_training_6_desc: Do multi-joint moves first when energy is highest, then finish with isolation work.
tip_training_7_desc: You can't improve what you don't measure. Log sets, reps, and weight every session.
tip_training_8_desc: Planned light weeks prevent overtraining and let your nervous system fully recover.
tip_training_9_desc: Legs are your largest muscle groups. Training them releases more anabolic hormones.
tip_training_10_desc: 5–10 minutes of light movement reduces lactic acid and next-day soreness.
tip_nutrition_1_title: Drink water before you feel thirsty
tip_nutrition_1_desc: Thirst is already mild dehydration. Aim for 500ml 30 minutes before every session.
tip_nutrition_2_desc: Muscles are most receptive to amino acids right after training. A shake works.
tip_nutrition_3_desc: Carbs are your primary fuel. A banana or oats 1 hour before gives sustained energy.
tip_nutrition_4_desc: This range consistently maximizes muscle protein synthesis. Split across 3–5 meals.
tip_nutrition_5_desc: You cannot build muscle in a significant deficit. If you're not growing, eat more.
tip_nutrition_6_desc: 80% whole foods ensures you get the micronutrients that support performance.
tip_nutrition_7_desc: Healthy fats support testosterone and joint health. Avocado, nuts, olive oil.
tip_nutrition_8_title: Creatine is the most proven supplement
tip_nutrition_8_desc: 5g daily of creatine monohydrate has decades of research for strength and gains.
tip_nutrition_9_title: Meal prep saves you from bad choices
tip_nutrition_9_desc: When tired and hungry, convenience wins. Prepped meals remove the decision entirely.
tip_recovery_1_title: Sleep is when you actually grow
tip_recovery_1_desc: Muscle repair and hormone production peak during deep sleep. Aim for 7–9 hours.
tip_recovery_2_desc: Light walking on rest days improves blood flow and reduces next-day soreness.
tip_recovery_3_desc: 2 minutes of cold water after training lowers inflammation and speeds repair.
tip_recovery_4_desc: Foam rolling first releases tight fascia so stretches actually reach the muscle.
tip_recovery_5_desc: Chronic stress elevates cortisol, which breaks down muscle tissue over time.
tip_recovery_6_title: Two rest days per muscle group minimum
tip_recovery_6_desc: Muscle protein synthesis takes 48–72 hours. Don't train the same muscle too soon.
tip_recovery_7_desc: 300–400mg before bed reduces muscle cramps and deepens sleep cycles.
tip_recovery_8_desc: Your muscles are 75% water. Under-hydration reduces strength output by up to 10%.
tip_mindset_1_desc: One missed day doesn't erase a week of effort. Celebrate small weekly wins.
tip_mindset_2_title: Set process goals, not just outcomes
tip_mindset_2_desc: 'Work out 3x a week' is more actionable than 'lose 10kg'. Process goals keep you moving.
tip_mindset_3_desc: Plateaus aren't failure — they're your body adapting. Change one variable to break through.
tip_mindset_4_desc: Don't wait to feel motivated. Start. Motivation appears after the first few minutes.
tip_mindset_5_desc: Your only competition is who you were 30 days ago. Everyone starts at a different point.
tip_mindset_6_desc: 'I am someone who trains' is more powerful than 'I'm trying to train'.
tip_mindset_7_desc: Mentally rehearse lifts before you do them. It activates real neural pathways.
tip_lifestyle_1_desc: Prolonged sitting negates gym gains. Walk for 2 minutes every 45 minutes.
tip_lifestyle_2_desc: 10 minutes of sunlight within 30 minutes of waking sets your circadian rhythm.
tip_lifestyle_3_desc: Even moderate drinking after training reduces MPS by up to 37%.
tip_lifestyle_4_desc: Blue light suppresses melatonin. Switch to night mode 1 hour before sleeping.
tip_lifestyle_5_desc: Daily step count is strongly linked to fat loss, independent of gym sessions.
tip_lifestyle_6_title: Find a training partner or community
tip_lifestyle_6_desc: Social accountability doubles workout consistency. Even an online group works.
